marketState: neutral, tilting bullish (hot cooling demand + extreme-short COT vs storage surplus and bearish daily/weekly trend)
summary: Price is running well below its seasonal median (-15.8% YTD vs -6.9% median, vsMedian -8.9pp) with low seasonality reliability (59pp 10-90% spread), and the probHigher odds actually fade from 56% at 2 weeks to 38% at 3-4 weeks, so seasonality alone is not a clean buy signal. Weather is the more decisive input: 8-16D demand is +20.5% vs the 5-year norm and near the top of its range (rangePos 0.86), building on a milder +8% 1-7D reading, which supports near-term gas burn. The fair-value model has front price essentially fair now (spread -$0.056) but fair value drifts higher through +21D ($2.923 to $3.028) while front stays flat, quietly widening the discount, and managed-money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range - a stretched, contrarian-bullish positioning skew. Working against that, storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise this week, and daily/weekly technicals remain bearish (EMA50<200, RSI in the mid-30s). Net-net this favors a patient long-term long on weather/COT/seasonal-turn-into-September, but no clean short-term edge yet since front price sits right at fair value with mixed intraday technicals.

longTerm.entry: $2.75-$2.85 (scale in on dips toward fair-value now-band lower half)
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +20.5% vs 5y near the top of its range, fair value drifts up through +21D while front price lags (widening the model discount), and managed-money shorts sit at a 5y-extreme 8th percentile raising squeeze risk; the 3-12 week window also carries into September, historically the strongest month (+3.1% median) after the seasonally weakest reading of the year in January.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus widens further with repeated bearish builds, 8-16D weather demand rolls back toward/below 5y normal, or price closes below the fair-value band low (~$2.42) confirming the bearish daily/weekly trend dominates.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially at fair value now (spread -$0.056, status FAIR) and sub-daily technicals conflict (5m/15m neutral, 1h/4h read bearish trend but positive MACD histogram), while the futures curve is nearly flat front-to-next (-$0.018 to Sep). The supportive 1-7D weather reading (+8% vs 5y, rangePos 0.67) is not extreme enough on its own to justify a directional trade over 1-10 days.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a 1h/4h close back above the recent range with MACD/EMA turning bullish to confirm a long, or a break below ~$2.75 with 1h/4h bearish EMA and negative MACD confirming downside continuation for a short.

keyDrivers: 8-16D cooling demand +20.5% vs 5y, rangePos 0.86 (near top of range) - bullish gas burn
Managed-money net short at 5y 8th percentile - contrarian short-covering risk
Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week - caps rallies
Fair-value model drifting up ($2.923 to $3.028 out to +21D) while front price lags - widening discount
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (EMA 50<200, RSI mid-30s) - higher-timeframe trend still down
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (59pp 10-90% spread) and probHigher fades to 38% at 3-4 weeks - the seasonal read could easily invert
Storage surplus could keep expanding if production stays strong, pressuring price despite hot weather
8-16D weather forecasts can shift quickly, reversing the current demand tailwind
